summary: The Docker-based reproduction bundle reaches the pinned vulnerable commit and the NetTest endpoint, but the final harness run cannot mint a servlet session over HTTP. Every candidate web-UI bootstrap route returned `404`, and direct upload without a session was rejected with `403`, so the oversized-body condition was not exercised end-to-end in this standalone harness.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should obtain a valid web session and then demonstrate that `POST /openmeetings/services/networktest/?size=1` accepts a body larger than the configured 2.5 MiB ceiling by returning an `accepted_bytes` proof value above 2621440.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `41677b22f9ba4236ac890332da823821065a5ca8`, built the reproduction image, started the application, and executed the targeted integration test. The test observed `404` for all candidate Wicket and mounted UI routes used to mint `JSESSIONID`, then confirmed that direct upload to `/openmeetings/services/networktest/?size=1` without a session returns `403`. No proof file was produced, so the oversized-upload acceptance was not verified in the standalone harness.
verification_method: The bundle copies `TestNetTestService.java` into the pinned checkout and runs `verify.sh`, which executes a targeted Maven integration test. Success requires the test to create `openmeetings-web/target/evohunt-nettest-proof.txt` with an `accepted_bytes` value above 2621440; failure is distinguished from generic startup issues by checking the exact bootstrap-route statuses and the direct upload status when no session exists.
evidence_produced_by_run: The final run reached the application and exercised the NetTest route under the pinned commit `41677b22f9ba4236ac890332da823821065a5ca8`.
The same failing assertion recorded that direct upload without a session returned `403`, matching the `request.getSession(false)` gate in `RateLimitRequestFilter`.
The Maven reactor completed through `openmeetings-webservice` and failed only in the final targeted test step, ruling out a generic clone, Docker, or startup failure.
limitations_or_blockers: The standalone test harness used by the reproduction bundle did not expose any reachable HTTP route that created a servlet `HttpSession`/`JSESSIONID`, even though Wicket initialized successfully.

Because `/openmeetings/services/networktest/` rejects requests without an existing session (`403`), the final run could not drive the vulnerable oversized-upload path far enough to produce the `accepted_bytes` proof artifact.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
